assertions.reason: Using only the saved resume source, Cover letter generation opened the draft modal and auto-saved a visible V1 entry for the MariaDB role.
Submitting refinement instructions created a new V2 version while preserving V1 in the same saved-history stack.
Selecting the saved V1 entry reopened the original V1 text snapshot in the modal after V2 already existed, proving stored draft snapshots can be reopened from history.

ATS analysis started automatically for generated text, stayed inside the draft modal when the ATS scorer returned a recoverable parser error, and re-entered analyzing state when the active saved version changed back to V1.
After reload, the MariaDB drawer still showed two saved cover-letter versions and the reopened draft modal preserved the same two-entry history.
Generation succeeded after LinkedIn and AI dump were both cleared, demonstrating that a single supported profile source (the saved resume) satisfied the minimum generation requirement.

The generation run routed through Gemini, with the browser session issuing POST requests to generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash:generateContent during both initial generation and refinement.
The same MariaDB saved drafts were visible from the drawer Draft studio surface and from the modal version history after reload, keeping card/drawer and modal history in sync.

frictions: ATS scoring remained in-flow but the local scorer returned recoverable Gemini JSON parse errors instead of a final scorecard.
agent-browser network filtering did not surface the request subset reliably, so the evidence uses the full recorded request log observed in-session.
summary: Round 4 unlocked the previously blocked draft-generation/history surface. Resume-only generation, refinement/versioning, reopening saved drafts, ATS lifecycle-in-modal behavior, history persistence after reload, Gemini transport routing, and drawer↔modal saved-draft visibility all passed.
